title: Human-machine interface with imaging application
abstract: A human-machine interface (HMI) (21), HMI system (2), turbomachinery package (1), and method of modifying a partition of an HMI (21) are disclosed. The HMI (21) comprises a first partition (211) storing a first operating system exclusively supporting a primary application; and a second partition (212) storing a second operating system exclusively supporting an imaging application. The HMI system (2) comprises the HMI (21); an external computing device (22); a serial cable (23); and, optionally, a network cable (24). The turbomachinery package (1) comprises a housing (11); a gas turbine (12); a plurality of sensors (14); a plurality of actuators (15); and an HMI (21). The imaging application may be an image deploy function, an image back-up function, and/or an image restore function, any of which can be executed without the use of removable media.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A human-machine interface system (2) configured for imaging applications, comprising: a human-machine interface (21) having: a first partition (211) storing a first operating system supporting a primary application; and a second partition (212) storing a second operating system supporting an imaging application to modify the first partition (211), the imaging application being selected from the group consisting of: image deploy, image back-up and image restore, wherein the human-machine interface runs the first operating system at a separate time from the second operating system. 2. The human-machine interface system (2) according to claim 1, wherein the first operating system is a WindowsÂ®-based operating system and the second operating system is a LinuxÂ®-based system; and wherein the humanmachine interface (21) boots from the first partition (211) storing the first operating system during a normal operation. 3. The human-machine interface system (2) according to claim 1, further comprising: an input device (213) operatively connected to the human-machine interface (21); and an output device (213) operatively connected to the humanmachine interface (21). 4. The human-machine interface system (2) according to claim 1, further comprising: an external computing device (22) having an input device (223) and an output device (224); and 

 a serial cable (23) connecting the human-machine interface (21) and the external computing device (22), the serial cable (23) being configured to transfer an input or an output. 5. The human-machine interface system (2) according to claim 1, further comprising: an external computing device (22) having an input device (223) and an output device (224); and a network cable (24) connecting the human-machine interface (21) and the external computing device (22), the network cable (24) being configured to transfer an image. 6. The human-machine interface system (2) according to claim 1, further comprising: an external computing device (22) having an input device (223), an output device (224), and an external networking unit (225); and an HMI networking unit (215). 7. A method of modifying a partition of a human-machine interface (21), comprising: providing a human-machine interface (21) having a first partition (211) and a second partition (212); configuring the first partition (211) to store a first operating system supporting a primary application; configuring the second partition (212) to store a second operating system supporting an imaging application to modify the first partition, the imaging application being selected from the group consisting of: image deploy, image back-up and image restore; encrypting the second partition (212); and booting from the first partition (211) storing the first operating system during a normal operation. 

 -18- 8. The method of modifying according to claim 7, further comprising: providing an input device (213) and an output device (214); and connecting, operatively, the input device (213) and the output device (214) to the human-machine interface (21). 9. The method of modifying according to claim 7, further comprising: providing an external computing device (22) having an input device (223) and an output device (224); providing a serial cable (23) configured to transfer an input or an output and/or providing a network cable (24) configured to transfer an image; connecting the human-machine interface (21) and the external computing device (22) via the serial cable (23) and/or the network cable (24); encrypting a serial cable (23) connection and/or a network cable (24) connection. 10. The method of modifying according to claim 7, further comprising: booting from the second partition (212) storing the second operating system; and running the imaging application.
